candidates.comment: The new mm_data forwarding block checks `prompt_images is not None` to decide whether to forward multimodal data. However, if `mm_data.get('image')` returns an empty list `[]`, the check `[] is not None` is True, so the code forwards `{'image': []}` to the diffusion stage — an empty image list that may cause downstream failures. The GLM-Image bridge's `_first_source_image` helper (in `glm_image.py`) handles this with `isinstance(image, list): return image[0] if image else None`, which returns None for empty lists. Add a similar guard: check both `is not None` AND truthiness (or `len()`) before forwarding.
candidates.evidence: Read `glm_image.py` lines 39-54 showing `_first_source_image` returns `image[0] if image else None` for lists; diff at hunyuan_image3.py lines ~207-213 shows the new block uses only `is not None` checks and does not guard against empty lists.

candidates.comment: The new `ar2diffusion` signature omits the `streaming_context: Any | None = None` parameter that the GLM-Image bridge (`glm_image.py` line 219) accepts. If the orchestrator ever passes `streaming_context` uniformly to all stage-input processors, this will raise `TypeError: ar2diffusion() got an unexpected keyword argument 'streaming_context'`. Consider adding the parameter for signature consistency (it can be accepted and ignored with `del streaming_context` like GLM-Image does).
candidates.evidence: Read `glm_image.py` line 219 showing `streaming_context: Any | None = None` in its `ar2diffusion` signature; hunyuan_image3.py line 94-97 shows the new signature without this parameter.

candidates.comment: The bridge relies on the orchestrator placing the parent output at `source_outputs[0]` (documented in the docstring at lines 101-103). This ordering is an inter-component invariant; consider adding an `assert source_outputs[0]` guard to catch regressions if the orchestrator changes.

The `requires_multimodal_data` parameter is accepted in the function signature but never referenced in the body — multimodal data is always forwarded unconditionally. The glm_image.ar2diffusion counterpart gates its multimodal forwarding on this parameter (glm_image.py:381 `if requires_multimodal_data:`). Either use the parameter consistently (gating the `mm_data` block) or remove it from the signature and docstring to avoid misleading callers. Given this PR is refactoring the function, it's the right moment to align.
candidates.evidence: Read full function body of hunyuan_image3.ar2diffusion at /rebase/vllm-omni/vllm_omni/model_executor/stage_input_processors/hunyuan_image3.py:96-213 — no reference to `requires_multimodal_data`. Compared with glm_image.ar2diffusion at /rebase/vllm-omni/vllm_omni/model_executor/stage_input_processors/glm_image.py:381 which gates `if requires_multimodal_data:` for the pil_image forwarding block.

candidates.comment: The auto-generated API docs reflect the old return type (list of dicts). After this PR, ar2diffusion returns `dict[str, Any] | None` instead of `list[dict[str, Any]]`. Regenerate the site docs before release (or add a note in the PR that docs will be regenerated separately).
candidates.evidence: grep for ar2diffusion in the repo shows site/api/vllm_omni/model_executor/stage_input_processors/hunyuan_image3/index.html exists as auto-generated documentation; the PR does not regenerate it. The function signature change from `list[dict[str, Any]]` to `dict[str, Any] | None` is in the diff at hunyuan_image3.py:98.

candidates.comment: The `_call_ar2diffusion` docstring says `Returns the diffusion request payload so callers can assert on the resolved height/width.` but its return type annotation is `dict[str, Any] | None`. The docstring should mention the `None` return case (`...or None when no parent output is available`) for parity with the main `ar2diffusion` function's docstring, even though this test helper never triggers the None path (it always passes at least one source output).
candidates.evidence: Read test_multi_resolution.py lines 523-531: docstring says 'Returns the diffusion request payload' while return type annotation is `dict[str, Any] | None`. The main ar2diffusion docstring in hunyuan_image3.py line 114 correctly says 'or ``None`` when no parent output is available.'

candidates.comment: Verification ask: This PR changes the `ar2diffusion` return contract from `list[dict]` to `dict | None` and claims the e2e test `test_image_to_image_alignment_online` (line 414) passed locally. However, no CI trace or output values (PSNR, SSIM, COT similarity, prefix match counts) appear in the PR description or gate report. This test exercises the exact changed path (AR→DiT bridge via `ar2diffusion`) and guards against image-quality and CoT-content regressions. Please attach the actual metric values from the run (the tabulate table printed by the test) to confirm the fix doesn't silently degrade output quality. The offline variant `test_image_to_image_alignment` (line ~480 in same file) should also be validated for the same reason.
candidates.evidence: Read tests/e2e/accuracy/test_hunyuan_image3.py lines 414-470; test validates PSNR >= 12.5 dB, SSIM >= 0.26, CLIP score >= 90, COT semantic similarity >= 0.9, and COT structural markers. The test calls `_run_online()` which invokes the full pipeline including the HunyuanImage3 `ar2diffusion` bridge. PR description states it 'passed without the list-prompt error' but provides no metric values.

candidates.comment: The new test `test_ar2diffusion_uses_parent_output_when_companions_are_present` (line 58) verifies the parent's `ar_generated_text` is used when companions exist, but it doesn't assert that companion-specific data (e.g. height/width from the companion's prompt) did NOT leak into the returned payload. Consider adding assertions on `result['height']`, `result['width']`, or other fields to confirm the companion output's data is fully excluded from the diffusion payload—this would strengthen the regression guard for the core fix (only parent output, not CFG companions, should drive the diffusion request).
candidates.evidence: Read tests/model_executor/stage_input_processors/test_hunyuan_image3_bridge.py lines 58-68; the test passes `[_source_output([100], text='parent thought'), _source_output([200], text='companion thought')]` with `prompt={'prompt': 'edit'}` and only asserts `result['extra']['ar_generated_text'] == 'parent thought'`. The companion output's data is silently ignored by the bridge (correctly) but the test doesn't prove it.
